**John Dodson** (born September 26, 1984) is an American [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist") and [bare-knuckle boxer](/wiki/Bare-knuckle_boxing "Bare-knuckle boxing") who competes in the flyweight divisions of [Rizin Fighting Federation](/wiki/Rizin_Fighting_Federation "Rizin Fighting Federation") and [Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship](/wiki/Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship "Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship"), where in the latter promotion, he is the inaugural and current [BKFC Flyweight Champion](/wiki/Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship#Flyweight_Championship "Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship"). A professional mixed martial arts competitor since 2004, Dodson has made a name for himself fighting mainly in the Southwest region. He was the winner of the [Spike TV](/wiki/Spike_TV "Spike TV")'s [The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Bisping_vs._Team_Miller "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller") and competed in [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") where he was a two-time title challenger, competing most recently in the bantamweight division, and is a veteran of the promotion with nine years of competition.

## Early and personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Dodson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early and personal life")]

Dodson was born in [Albuquerque, New Mexico](/wiki/Albuquerque,_New_Mexico "Albuquerque, New Mexico"). He grew up in Albuquerque and later moved to [Edgewood, New Mexico](/wiki/Edgewood,_New_Mexico "Edgewood, New Mexico") with his family. Dodson is of [African American](/wiki/African_American "African American") and [Filipino](/wiki/Filipino_Americans "Filipino Americans") descent.[[3]](#cite_note-3) He attended Moriarty High School in [Moriarty, New Mexico](/wiki/Moriarty,_New_Mexico "Moriarty, New Mexico").[[4]](#cite_note-Dodson1-4) While attending Moriarty High School, Dodson competed in [track and field](/wiki/Track_and_field "Track and field"), [football](/wiki/American_football "American football") and [amateur wrestling](/wiki/Amateur_wrestling "Amateur wrestling"). In [wrestling](/wiki/Scholastic_wrestling "Scholastic wrestling"), Dodson was a two-time state champion, one time state runner-up and four time district champion.[[5]](#cite_note-Dodson2-5) Dodson graduated from high school in 2003, and then enrolled at the [University of New Mexico](/wiki/University_of_New_Mexico "University of New Mexico") while working at a local [Chuck E. Cheese's](/wiki/Chuck_E._Cheese%27s "Chuck E. Cheese's") restaurant. However, he did not earn a degree.[[4]](#cite_note-Dodson1-4)[[5]](#cite_note-Dodson2-5)[[6]](#cite_note-Myspace-6)

Dodson was raised by a single mother who hails from the [Philippines](/wiki/Philippines "Philippines"), along with his brother, Eric. His favorite Filipino food is [Pancit](/wiki/Pancit "Pancit").[[7]](#cite_note-7) Dodson has daughters named Delilah Skye and Aurora Rose with his wife Chelsea.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Dodson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

Dodson began training MMA in 2002 after Chris Luttrell and [Greg Jackson](/wiki/Greg_Jackson_(MMA_trainer) "Greg Jackson (MMA trainer)") recruited him for his extensive wrestling background. After the first day of training, he "fell in love with the sport" and continued to train daily with Jackson.[[5]](#cite_note-Dodson2-5)

Dodson competed in a Grappler's Quest and won in the beginner level. Four months later, he competed in another submission tournament, winning at the intermediate level. Six months later, Dodson competed in his first amateur fight. He competed in one other amateur bout before turning pro.[[5]](#cite_note-Dodson2-5) Dodson was offered to make his debut in Japan against the number one ranked flyweight in the world, [Yasuhiro Urushitani](/wiki/Yasuhiro_Urushitani "Yasuhiro Urushitani").[[5]](#cite_note-Dodson2-5) Dodson lost the fight via decision.[[10]](#cite_note-Fightfinder-10)

With the lack of fights materializing at flyweight, Dodson began taking fights at featherweight and bantamweight as well. He obtained a 3–2 record before taking a fight against future WEC fighter, Clint Godfrey, winning the fight via decision. With the win over Godfrey, Dodson was selected to be a part of the second season of the Tapout Reality show.[[11]](#cite_note-TapOut-11)

Despite having a 1–1 record under the Ultimate Warrior Challenge promotion, he was awarded shot at the vacant UWC flyweight title at UWC 7. Dodson was defeated by Pat Runez in a highly controversial five-round decision.[[12]](#cite_note-12) At UWC 8, Dodson defeated previously unbeaten Jesse Riggleman via unanimous decision.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Dodson was expected to fight at [Tachi Palace Fights 9](/wiki/Tachi_Palace_Fights_9 "Tachi Palace Fights 9") against [Alexis Vila](/wiki/Alexis_Vila "Alexis Vila").[[14]](#cite_note-14) However, Vila suffered an injury and was forced to pull out of the bout. Vila was replaced by former Shooto champion [Mamoru Yamaguchi](/wiki/Mamoru_Yamaguchi "Mamoru Yamaguchi").[[15]](#cite_note-15) Dodson then pulled out of the fight for unknown reasons, and was replaced by Kevin Dunsmoor.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

### The Ultimate Fighter

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Dodson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: The Ultimate Fighter")]

In 2011, Dodson had signed with the [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") to compete on [The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Bisping_vs._Team_Miller "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller"). In the first episode, Dodson fought Brandon Merkt to gain entry into the *Ultimate Fighter* house. Dodson defeated Merkt in the first round via TKO.

Dodson was selected as a part of Team Mayhem, he was the first bantamweight chosen for the team (fourth overall). Dodson defeated [John Albert](/wiki/John_Albert_(fighter) "John Albert (fighter)") via unanimous decision in the preliminary round and moved onto the semi-finals.

In the semi-finals Dodson was selected to fight Team Mayhem teammate, [Johnny Bedford](/wiki/Johnny_Bedford "Johnny Bedford"). After a close first round, Dodson won the fight via KO in the first minute of the second round. The win moved Dodson into the finals. The impressive finish also won Dodson an additional $25,000 for the fan voted "Knockout of the Season".[[17]](#cite_note-17)

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Dodson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

Dodson made his UFC debut on December 3, 2011, at [The Ultimate Fighter 14 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_14_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter 14 Finale") against [T.J. Dillashaw](/wiki/T.J._Dillashaw "T.J. Dillashaw") to determine the bantamweight winner of [The Ultimate Fighter 14](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_14 "The Ultimate Fighter 14"). Dodson won the fight via TKO in the first round to become the first Bantamweight winner of [The Ultimate Fighter](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter "The Ultimate Fighter") competition. He received an additional $40,000 for winning *Knockout of the Night* honors.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

For his next fight, Dodson moved back down to the flyweight division and was expected to face [Darren Uyenoyama](/wiki/Darren_Uyenoyama "Darren Uyenoyama") on May 5, 2012, at [UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3").[[19]](#cite_note-19) However, Uyenoyama was forced out of the bout, and was replaced by promotional newcomer [Tim Elliott](/wiki/Tim_Elliott "Tim Elliott").[[20]](#cite_note-20) Dodson defeated Elliott by unanimous decision, generating crowd booing.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

Dodson faced promotional newcomer [Jussier Formiga](/wiki/Jussier_Formiga "Jussier Formiga") on October 5, 2012, at [UFC on FX 5](/wiki/UFC_on_FX_5 "UFC on FX 5").[[22]](#cite_note-22) He won the fight via TKO in the second round.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

#### First title shot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Dodson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: First title shot")]

Dodson faced UFC Flyweight champion [Demetrious Johnson](/wiki/Demetrious_Johnson_(fighter) "Demetrious Johnson (fighter)") on January 26, 2013, at [UFC on Fox 6](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_6 "UFC on Fox 6"),[[24]](#cite_note-24) becoming the first Ultimate Fighter alumnus to fight for a title post-season 5. Despite knocking Johnson down twice in the second round, Dodson lost the fight via unanimous decision in a bout that earned both participants *Fight of the Night* honors.[[25]](#cite_note-25)[[26]](#cite_note-26)

Dodson faced promotional newcomer [Darrell Montague](/wiki/Darrell_Montague "Darrell Montague") on October 19, 2013, at [UFC 166](/wiki/UFC_166 "UFC 166").[[27]](#cite_note-27) He won via knockout at 4:13 of the first round.[[28]](#cite_note-28) The win also earned him his second *Knockout of the Night* award.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

Dodson was expected to face [Scott Jorgensen](/wiki/Scott_Jorgensen "Scott Jorgensen") on December 14, 2013, at [UFC on Fox 9](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_9 "UFC on Fox 9"), replacing [Ian McCall](/wiki/Ian_McCall_(fighter) "Ian McCall (fighter)").[[30]](#cite_note-30) However, Dodson himself was forced from the bout after sustaining a knee injury.[[31]](#cite_note-31) Jorgensen instead faced promotional newcomer [Zach Makovsky](/wiki/Zach_Makovsky "Zach Makovsky").[[32]](#cite_note-MakovskyIn-32)

A rematch with [John Moraga](/wiki/John_Moraga "John Moraga") took place on June 7, 2014, at [UFC Fight Night 42](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_42 "UFC Fight Night 42").[[33]](#cite_note-33) Dodson won the fight via TKO due to a doctor stoppage between rounds two and three after a knee he threw late in the second round.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

Dodson faced [Zach Makovsky](/wiki/Zach_Makovsky "Zach Makovsky") on May 23, 2015, at [UFC 187](/wiki/UFC_187 "UFC 187").[[35]](#cite_note-35) Dodson won the fight via unanimous decision.[[36]](#cite_note-36)

#### Second title shot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Dodson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Second title shot")]

A rematch with the Flyweight champion [Demetrious Johnson](/wiki/Demetrious_Johnson_(fighter) "Demetrious Johnson (fighter)") took place on September 5, 2015, at [UFC 191](/wiki/UFC_191 "UFC 191").[[37]](#cite_note-37) In a completely one-sided affair, Dodson lost the fight via unanimous decision.[[38]](#cite_note-38)

#### Return to Bantamweight

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Dodson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Return to Bantamweight")]

Dodson faced [Manvel Gamburyan](/wiki/Manvel_Gamburyan "Manvel Gamburyan") in a bantamweight bout on April 16, 2016, at [UFC on Fox 19](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_19 "UFC on Fox 19"),[[39]](#cite_note-39) He won the fight via TKO in the first round.[[40]](#cite_note-40)

Dodson faced [John Lineker](/wiki/John_Lineker "John Lineker") on October 1, 2016, at [UFC Fight Night 96](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_96 "UFC Fight Night 96").[[41]](#cite_note-41) The bout took place at a catchweight of 136.5 lbs as Lineker missed weight. He lost the back and forth fight via split decision.[[42]](#cite_note-42)

Dodson faced [Eddie Wineland](/wiki/Eddie_Wineland "Eddie Wineland") on April 22, 2017, at [UFC Fight Night 108](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_108 "UFC Fight Night 108").[[43]](#cite_note-43) He won the fight via unanimous decision.[[44]](#cite_note-44)

Dodson faced [Marlon Moraes](/wiki/Marlon_Moraes "Marlon Moraes") on November 11, 2017, at [UFC Fight Night 120](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_120 "UFC Fight Night 120").[[45]](#cite_note-45) He lost the fight via split decision.[[46]](#cite_note-46)

Dodson was expected to face [Pedro Munhoz](/wiki/Pedro_Munhoz "Pedro Munhoz") at [UFC Fight Night 125](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_125 "UFC Fight Night 125") on February 3, 2018.[[47]](#cite_note-47) However, Munhoz missed weight by four pounds over the bantamweight non-title fight upper limit of 136 pounds, negotiations fell through, and the fight was cancelled.[[48]](#cite_note-48) The fight was rescheduled to [UFC 222](/wiki/UFC_222 "UFC 222") on March 3, 2018.[[49]](#cite_note-49) Dodson won the bout via split decision.[[50]](#cite_note-50)

Dodson's contract with UFC expired after the fight with Munhoz and after listening to offers from various companies, he re-signed a new four-fight with UFC.[[51]](#cite_note-51)

Dodson faced [Jimmie Rivera](/wiki/Jimmie_Rivera "Jimmie Rivera") on September 8, 2018, at [UFC 228](/wiki/UFC_228 "UFC 228").[[52]](#cite_note-52) He lost the fight via unanimous decision.[[53]](#cite_note-53)

Dodson faced [Petr Yan](/wiki/Petr_Yan "Petr Yan") on February 23, 2019, at [UFC Fight Night 145](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_145 "UFC Fight Night 145").[[54]](#cite_note-54) He lost the fight by unanimous decision.[[55]](#cite_note-55)

Dodson faced [Nathaniel Wood](/wiki/Nathaniel_Wood_(fighter) "Nathaniel Wood (fighter)") on February 15, 2020, at [UFC Fight Night 167](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_167 "UFC Fight Night 167").[[56]](#cite_note-56) He won the fight via TKO in the third round.[[57]](#cite_note-57)

On June 16, 2020, news surfaced that with one fight left on his prevailing contract, Dodson re-signed a five-fight deal with the UFC.[[58]](#cite_note-58)

Dodson faced [Merab Dvalishvili](/wiki/Merab_Dvalishvili "Merab Dvalishvili") on August 15, 2020, at [UFC 252](/wiki/UFC_252 "UFC 252").[[59]](#cite_note-59) He lost the fight via unanimous decision.[[60]](#cite_note-60)

On September 18, 2020, it was announced that Dodson was released by UFC.[[61]](#cite_note-61)

### Post UFC

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Dodson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Post UFC")]

Dodson was scheduled to fight his first bout after release on July 30, 2021, at XMMA 2 against [Cody Gibson](/wiki/Cody_Gibson "Cody Gibson").[[62]](#cite_note-62) However, Dodson got into a potentially life-threatening car accident with his family and withdrew from the bout.[[63]](#cite_note-63) Gibson knocked out replacement Louis Sanoudakis in 44 seconds.[[64]](#cite_note-64) Dodson's bout with Gibson was rescheduled for XMMA 3 on October 23.[[65]](#cite_note-65) Dodson lost the fight by unanimous decision.[[66]](#cite_note-66)

Dodson faced [Francisco Rivera](/wiki/Francisco_Rivera_(fighter) "Francisco Rivera (fighter)") on April 2, 2022, at XMMA 4.[[67]](#cite_note-67) He won the bout via unanimous decision.[[68]](#cite_note-68)

Dodson signed a multi-fight contract with [Rizin Fighting Federation](/wiki/Rizin_Fighting_Federation "Rizin Fighting Federation") and debuted against [Hideo Tokoro](/wiki/Hideo_Tokoro "Hideo Tokoro") in a return to flyweight on December 31, 2022, at [Rizin 40](/wiki/Rizin_40 "Rizin 40").[[69]](#cite_note-69) He won the bout by knockout in the first round via punches.[[70]](#cite_note-70)

Dodson faced Tatsuki Saomoto at [Rizin 42](/wiki/2023_in_Rizin_Fighting_Federation#Rizin_42 "2023 in Rizin Fighting Federation") on May 6, 2023. He won the fight by unanimous decision.[[71]](#cite_note-71)

Dodson faced [Hiromasa Ougikubo](/wiki/Hiromasa_Ougikubo "Hiromasa Ougikubo") on December 31, 2023, at [Rizin 45](/wiki/2023_in_Rizin_Fighting_Federation#Rizin_45 "2023 in Rizin Fighting Federation").[[72]](#cite_note-72) and lost the bout via unanimous decision.[[73]](#cite_note-73)

## Bare-knuckle boxing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Dodson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Bare-knuckle boxing career")]

### Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Dodson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship")]

In June 2022, it was announced that Dodson signed with [Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship](/wiki/Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship "Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship").[[74]](#cite_note-74)

Dodson faced [Ryan Benoit](/wiki/Ryan_Benoit "Ryan Benoit") on August 27, 2022, at [BKFC 28](/wiki/2022_in_Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship#BKFC_28_Albuquerque:_Ferea_vs._Starling "2022 in Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship"). Dodson won the fight via knockout in the first round.[[75]](#cite_note-75) This fight earned him the *Performance of the Night* award.[[76]](#cite_note-bkfc28-76)

Dodson fought [Jarod Grant](/wiki/Jarod_Grant "Jarod Grant") on February 17, 2023, at [BKFC KnuckleMania 3](/wiki/2023_in_Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship#BKFC_KnuckleMania_3 "2023 in Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship"). He won the bout by first round knockout. [[77]](#cite_note-77)

Dodson fought for the inaugural BKFC Flyweight championship on August 11, 2023, at [BKFC 48](/wiki/2023_in_Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship#BKFC_48_Albuquerque:_Dodson_vs._Ridge "2023 in Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship") and won by first round technical knockout.[[78]](#cite_note-bkfc48results-78)

On March 29, 2024 in Albuquerque, New Mexico, Dodson put his [BKFC Flyweight Championship](/wiki/Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship#Flyweight_Championship "Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship") on the line against Dagoberto Aguero in the main event of [BKFC 59](/wiki/2024_in_Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship#BKFC_59_Albuquerque:_Dodson_vs._Aguero "2024 in Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship").[[79]](#cite_note-79) The fight was ruled a unanimous draw.[[80]](#cite_note-80) This fight earned him the *Fight of the Night* award.[[81]](#cite_note-bkfc59-81)
